commits.author: kmransom56
commits.message:
feat: Add Docker cagent integration with web UI and golang_developer agent

Major improvements to AI Toolkit:

1. Web UI Cagent Integration:
   - Added /api/cagent/run endpoint for executing cagent examples
   - Fixed Windows path conversion bug (backslash to forward slash)
   - Fixed JSON serialization bug for Docker stderr output
   - Added API key passthrough (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini) to containers
   - Enforced dry-run mode for safety in web UI
   - Improved error handling and reporting

2. New Golang Developer Agent:
   - Created cagent_examples/golang_developer.yaml
   - Expert agent for Docker cagent development (dogfooding)
   - Specialized in Go, multi-agent systems, and cagent architecture
   - Includes filesystem, shell, web search, and git tools

3. Docker Compose Updates:
   - Added cagent service configuration
   - Integrated with existing AI toolkit services

4. Documentation:
   - Updated PowerInfer implementation summary
   - Added cagent usage examples

All changes tested and working. Ready for deployment on Tesla K80 machine.

commits.author: Bruno Verachten
commits.message:
fix(ci): add cagent and buildx to repository update triggers (#143)

* fix(ci): add cagent package builds to repository update triggers

Add "Build cagent Debian Package" to APT repo update workflow triggers
Add "Build cagent RPM Package" to RPM repo update workflow triggers
Add "Build Buildx RPM Package" to RPM repo update workflow triggers (was missing)

This ensures the APT and RPM repositories are automatically updated
when cagent (or buildx) packages are built and uploaded to releases.

Also updated workflow_dispatch description to include cagent releases.

* fix(ci): add cagent package download support to repository updates

APT repository (update-apt-repo.yml):
- Add logic to find latest cagent release
- Add cagent package download from cagent releases
- Add cagent to cleanup pattern list

RPM repository (update-rpm-repo.yml):
- Add cagent-v and buildx-v patterns to release detection regex
- This allows automatic repo updates when cagent/buildx RPM packages are built

Without these changes, cagent packages were not being downloaded
or added to the repositories even though the workflows were triggered.

* fix(ci): address CodeRabbit review feedback for APT repo workflow

Critical logic fixes:
- Add buildx- and cagent- to release tag detection regex (line 45)
- Add buildx and cagent package type detection (lines 57-62)
- Add buildx and cagent version extraction and naming (lines 254-261)

Code quality improvements:
- Split long workflow trigger list into multi-line array (lines 5-10)
- Use multi-line string for release_tag description (lines 16-18)
- Break long jq commands across multiple lines (all release detection queries)
- Remove trailing whitespace (line 274)

All changes ensure cagent and buildx packages are properly detected,
downloaded, and committed with correct version information.

Addresses all 5 CodeRabbit review comments on PR #143

commits.message:
fix(ci): add automatic tracking issue closure to cagent workflow (#271)

Add missing "Close tracking issue" step to cagent-weekly-build.yml to
automatically close tracking issues created by track-cagent-releases.yml
after successful builds.

Changes:
- Add issues:write permission
- Add "Close tracking issue" step that:
  - Searches for tracking issues by label (build-in-progress, cagent-release)
  - Comments with release link
  - Closes issue with "completed" reason
  - Only runs for official releases (not dev builds)

Manually closed 6 existing open cagent tracking issues (v1.16.0 through
v1.19.0) that were created before this automation was added.

This completes the tracking issue auto-close feature across all build
workflows (Docker, CLI, Compose, Tini, and now cagent).

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.author: Bruno Verachten
commits.message:
fix(ci): resolve BuildKit, cagent, and RPM build failures (#325)

* fix(ci): resolve BuildKit, cagent, and RPM build failures

- BuildKit: use default Docker driver instead of docker-container to
  avoid "exec /sbin/docker-init: no such file" error (tini missing in
  moby/buildkit:buildx-stable-1 for riscv64). Also clean up mybuilder
  (not just riscv-builder) in pre-build cleanup.

- cagent: bump Go version from 1.25.5 to 1.26.0 to match upstream
  go.mod requirement (go >= 1.26.0).

- RPM packages: sanitize version strings by replacing hyphens with dots
  (e.g. "20260215-dev" -> "20260215.dev") since RPM spec Version field
  cannot contain hyphens. Applied to all 6 RPM build workflows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): use own BuildKit image with tini instead of default driver

Pre-create mybuilder with ghcr.io/gounthar/buildkit-riscv64:latest
(which includes tini) instead of switching to the default driver.
This keeps the docker-container driver capabilities while fixing the
"exec /sbin/docker-init: no such file" error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): auto-detect Go version from cagent go.mod

Read Go version dynamically from cagent/go.mod at build time instead
of hardcoding it. This prevents build failures when upstream bumps
their Go requirement, matching the pattern already used by
docker-weekly-build.yml.

The UpdateCLI automation for cagent Go version tracking is now
redundant and can be removed in a follow-up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(ci): remove redundant UpdateCLI cagent Go version tracking

The cagent workflow now auto-detects Go version from go.mod at build
time, making the UpdateCLI-based Go version tracking unnecessary.

Removes:
- .updatecli.d/cagent-go-version.yaml config
- Go version step from updatecli-cagent.yml workflow

The cagent release version tracking (cagent.yaml) is kept.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): move RPM version validation before hyphen normalization

The validation check ($VERSION = $RELEASE_TAG) was running after
tr '-' '.', making it always pass since the transformed version
can never equal the original tag. Move validation before the tr
step in all 6 RPM workflows, and add it to the 4 that were missing
it entirely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.author: Bruno Verachten
commits.message:
feat(cagent): add cagent multi-agent AI runtime support (#140)

* feat(cagent): add cagent multi-agent AI runtime support

Add comprehensive support for building and packaging cagent, Docker's
multi-agent AI runtime, for RISC-V64 architecture.

This makes docker-for-riscv64 the first Linux distribution to provide
native cagent packages.

**Components Added:**
- cagent submodule (v1.9.13)
- Build script: build-cagent-riscv64.sh
- Debian packaging: debian-cagent/
- RPM packaging: rpm-cagent/

**Automation:**
- Weekly builds: cagent-weekly-build.yml (Sunday 06:00 UTC)
- Release tracking: track-cagent-releases.yml (Daily 08:00 UTC)
- Debian packaging: build-cagent-package.yml
- RPM packaging: build-cagent-rpm.yml
- UpdateCLI manifest: .updatecli.d/cagent.yaml

**What is cagent:**
Multi-agent AI runtime by Docker Engineering that orchestrates AI agents
with specialized capabilities. Features MCP integration, YAML-based
configuration, and support for multiple AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic,
Gemini, xAI, Mistral).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(updatecli): improve cagent manifest condition and changedif logic

Fix two issues identified in PR review:

1. **Condition Logic**: Exit 1 when binary already exists to skip
   unnecessary updates. Previously exited 0 which caused UpdateCLI
   to continue processing already-built versions.

2. **Changedif Optimization**: Use shell-based git comparison instead
   of checking .gitmodules file existence. This prevents redundant
   git operations by only updating when submodule commit changes.

Addresses review comments from @gemini-code-assist[bot] on PR #140.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address all 8 CodeRabbit review comments

Implement comprehensive fixes for all CodeRabbit-identified issues:

**Workflow Improvements:**
1. Extract duplicate release tag detection logic (build-cagent-package.yml)
   - Created reusable "Determine release tag" step with output
   - Eliminated 3 instances of duplicated detection code
   - Follows DRY principles

2. Fix branch detection (cagent-weekly-build.yml)
   - Use git symbolic-ref instead of git show-ref
   - Properly handles tags, commits, and branches
   - Prevents pull failures on detached HEAD

3. Add error handling (track-cagent-releases.yml)
   - Validate gh API responses with error checks
   - Exit cleanly on empty or failed responses
   - Added set -euo pipefail for strict error handling

4. Add concurrency control (track-cagent-releases.yml)
   - Prevent race conditions in release tracking
   - Ensure only one workflow instance runs at a time
   - Set cancel-in-progress: false to allow completion

**UpdateCLI Improvements:**
5. Improve condition error handling (cagent.yaml)
   - Validate gh CLI availability
   - Check upstream release existence explicitly
   - Clear exit codes and error messages

**Build Script Improvements:**
6. Add directory existence check (build-cagent-riscv64.sh)
   - Verify cagent submodule exists before cd
   - Provide helpful error message with init instructions

**Packaging Improvements:**
7. Fix Debian version format (debian-cagent/changelog)
   - Changed from 1.9.13-riscv64-1 to 1.9.13-1
   - Architecture belongs in control file, not version string
   - Follows Debian Policy standards

8. Update Standards-Version (debian-cagent/control)
   - Updated from 4.7.0 to 4.7.2.0
   - Aligns with latest Debian Policy (Feb 2025)

All fixes address legitimate technical issues identified by automated
review. No functionality changes, only quality improvements.

Addresses: PR #140 CodeRabbit review comments

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address final CodeRabbit review comments

Fix remaining issues identified in latest review:

**UpdateCLI Pattern Fix (Critical):**
- Fixed version pattern mismatch in cagent.yaml
- Changed matchpattern from '^cagent \(([0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+)-riscv64-1\)'
  to '^cagent \(([0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+)-1\)'
- Changed replacepattern to match new Debian-standard version format
- This was broken after we fixed the Debian version format in previous commit
- UpdateCLI will now correctly match and update the changelog

**VERSION Validation (Nitpick):**
- Added semantic versioning validation in build-cagent-package.yml
- Validates VERSION matches ^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$ pattern
- Prevents malformed changelog entries from unexpected tag formats
- Fails early with clear error message if VERSION is invalid

Both fixes ensure UpdateCLI automation and package building work
correctly with the Debian-standard version format (X.Y.Z-1).

Addresses: PR #140 CodeRabbit follow-up comments

commits.author: Bruno Verachten
commits.message:
feat(ci): add UpdateCLI automation for cagent Go version tracking (#165)

* feat(ci): add UpdateCLI automation for cagent Go version tracking

Automatically tracks the Go version required by cagent's go.mod and
updates the cagent-weekly-build.yml workflow when it changes.

This prevents build failures like the one seen in run 19635816761 where
cagent updated to Go 1.25.4 but the workflow was still using Go 1.23.

Components:
- .updatecli.d/cagent-go-version.yaml: Manifest that:
  - Reads Go version from cagent's go.mod (upstream)
  - Validates version format
  - Updates cagent-weekly-build.yml workflow
  - Creates PR with changes

- .github/workflows/updatecli-cagent.yml: Workflow that:
  - Runs daily at 09:00 UTC
  - Executes UpdateCLI with cagent manifest
  - Can be manually triggered

Benefits:
- Automatic detection of Go version updates in cagent
- Proactive PR creation before builds fail
- Reduces manual maintenance overhead

* fix(ci): address review feedback on UpdateCLI cagent manifest

Fixes identified by @gemini-code-assist and @Copilot:

1. Go Version Pattern Flexibility:
   - Updated matchpattern to support both X.Y and X.Y.Z formats
   - Go's go.mod commonly uses X.Y format (e.g., 'go 1.25')
   - Pattern: '^go\s+(\d+\.\d+(?:\.\d+)?)$'

2. SCM Configuration:
   - Added missing 'default' SCM for PR creation
   - Changed 'user' to 'owner' in cagent-upstream SCM
   - Follows pattern from gentoo-docker-cli.yaml

3. Correct Step Index:
   - Fixed YAML path from steps[1] to steps[2]
   - steps[0]=Checkout, steps[1]=Update submodule, steps[2]=Set up Go

4. Target Configuration:
   - Added scmid: 'default' to target section

5. Documentation:
   - Added warning comment in workflow about step ordering
   - Added explanatory comments in manifest

commits.author: caelan
commits.message:
feat: Initial cAgents v4.0.0 - Universal multi-domain agent system

This is the first release of cAgents (Caelan's Agents), a complete rebranding
and architectural evolution from agent-design.

## What's New

**Multi-Domain Architecture**
- Universal infrastructure (@cagents/core) with Trigger, Orchestrator, HITL
- Software engineering domain (@cagents/software) with 46 specialized agents
- Placeholder domains for creative writing, business ops, and more

**Core Features**
- Unified /trigger command routes to appropriate domain automatically
- File-based Agent_Memory system for stateful collaboration
- Complexity tiers (0-4) with automatic routing
- Team-based agent collaboration (consultation, review, delegation, escalation)

**Software Engineering Domain (46 Agents)**
- Workflow: Router, Planner, Executor, Validator, Self-Correct
- Executive: CEO, CTO, CFO, COO, VP Engineering
- Team: Product, Tech Lead, Architect, Senior Dev, Frontend, Backend, QA, Security, DevOps, DBA, etc.
- Intelligence Layer: Pattern Recognition, Risk Assessment, Dependency Analyzer
- QA Layer: Architecture Review, Code Standards, Security Analysis, Performance, etc.

## Installation

\`\`\`bash
claude /plugin install cagents
\`\`\`

## Migration from agent-design

This repository supersedes agent-design. Users of agent-design should migrate to cAgents.

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

docs(cagent): add model providers and rag guides

Add new documentation files covering cloud model provider configuration
and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for cagent.

- The model providers guide explains how to set up API keys and
  environment variables for Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google Gemini,
  as well as OpenAI-compatible providers.
- The RAG guide details how cagent indexes content, handles large
  context windows through semantic search, and automatically
  updates the index when files change.

This documentation helps users transition from local models to cloud
providers and explains the technical implementation of the agent's
search capabilities.

commits.author: Bruno Verachten
commits.message:
perf(ci): optimize all weekly build workflows to checkout only needed submodules (#203)

* perf(ci): only checkout cagent submodule in cagent workflow

Instead of cloning all submodules (moby ~800MB, cli, compose, cagent),
only init and update the cagent submodule. This significantly reduces
checkout time on the self-hosted RISC-V64 runner.

Changes:
- Set submodules: false in checkout action
- Use 'git submodule update --init --depth 1 cagent' for shallow clone
- Step order preserved (UpdateCLI automation still targets step[2])

* perf(ci): optimize cli, compose, and docker workflows

Apply the same submodule optimization to all weekly build workflows:
- cli-weekly-build.yml: only checkout cli submodule
- compose-weekly-build.yml: only checkout compose submodule
- docker-weekly-build.yml: only checkout moby submodule

Each workflow now uses shallow clone (--depth 1) for its required
submodule instead of cloning all submodules.

fix: Use CAGENTS_DIR env var for portable hook paths across projects (v9.10.4)

cAgents plugin directory, causing MODULE_NOT_FOUND when hooks run from
other projects. Replace with CAGENTS_DIR env var set to the actual
cAgents installation path. The install script now auto-sets this value.
run-hook.cjs prioritizes CAGENTS_DIR and __dirname for resolution.

commits.author: Bruno Verachten
commits.message:
fix(buildx): correct shell syntax error in VERSION_INFO assignment (#272)

* fix(ci): add automatic tracking issue closure to cagent workflow (#271)

Add missing "Close tracking issue" step to cagent-weekly-build.yml to
automatically close tracking issues created by track-cagent-releases.yml
after successful builds.

Changes:
- Add issues:write permission
- Add "Close tracking issue" step that:
  - Searches for tracking issues by label (build-in-progress, cagent-release)
  - Comments with release link
  - Closes issue with "completed" reason
  - Only runs for official releases (not dev builds)

Manually closed 6 existing open cagent tracking issues (v1.16.0 through
v1.19.0) that were created before this automation was added.

This completes the tracking issue auto-close feature across all build
workflows (Docker, CLI, Compose, Tini, and now cagent).

* fix(buildx): correct shell syntax error in VERSION_INFO assignment

Fix multi-line string assignment in buildx-weekly-build.yml that would
cause workflow failures for development builds.

The VERSION_INFO variable had a broken multi-line assignment where the
second line would be interpreted as a separate command instead of a
string continuation.

Changed from:
  VERSION_INFO="**Buildx Branch:** ${BUILDX_REF}
**Buildx Commit:** $(cd buildx && git rev-parse --short HEAD)"

To:
  VERSION_INFO="**Buildx Branch:** ${BUILDX_REF}"$'\n'"**Buildx Commit:** $(cd buildx && git rev-parse --short HEAD)"

This uses bash's $'\n' syntax to properly embed a newline in the string.

Fixes #92

commits.author: Bruno Verachten
commits.message:
fix(ci): update Go version to 1.25.5 and fix UpdateCLI workflows (#201)

* fix(ci): update Go version to 1.25.5 and fix UpdateCLI workflows

- Update cagent weekly build Go version from 1.25.4 to 1.25.5 to match
  upstream cagent go.mod requirement
- Fix UpdateCLI workflows that were missing command/config parameters
  and therefore not actually running any manifests
- Add values.yaml to provide template variables for UpdateCLI manifests
- UpdateCLI cagent workflow now runs both version and Go version tracking
- UpdateCLI gentoo workflow now properly runs all gentoo-*.yaml manifests

Fixes cagent build failure:
  go: go.mod requires go >= 1.25.5 (running go 1.25.4; GOTOOLCHAIN=local)

* docs: clarify token configuration in values.yaml

The token field holds the NAME of the environment variable, not the token
itself. The manifests use {{ requiredEnv .github.token }} which:
1. First resolves .github.token from values.yaml → "UPDATECLI_GITHUB_TOKEN"
2. Then requiredEnv reads the environment variable with that name

This is the intended design pattern for UpdateCLI.

* fix(updatecli): add missing token to cagent-upstream SCM

The cagent-upstream SCM was missing the token field, which would cause
UpdateCLI to fail when accessing the GitHub API. All GitHub SCMs need
a token for API access, even for public repositories.

commits.author: David Gageot
commits.message:
feat(alias): add --yolo and --model options to alias configuration

Aliases can now store runtime options that are automatically applied when
the alias is used:

- --yolo: automatically approve all tool calls without prompting
- --model: override the agent's model (format: [agent=]provider/model)

Examples:
  cagent alias add yolo-coder agentcatalog/coder --yolo
  cagent alias add fast-coder agentcatalog/coder --model openai/gpt-4o-mini
  cagent alias add turbo agentcatalog/coder --yolo --model anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-0

The alias list command now displays options in brackets:
  fast-coder → agentcatalog/coder [model=openai/gpt-4o-mini]
  turbo      → agentcatalog/coder [yolo, model=anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-0]

Command-line flags override alias options when explicitly set.

Assisted-By: cagent
